title: Towards homological mirror symmetry for complete intersections in toric varieties
speaker: Denis Auroux
abstract: Abstract: In this talk we will report on joint work in progress with Mohammed Abouzaid concerning homological mirror symmetry for hypersurfaces in (C*)^n, namely, comparing the derived category of the hypersurface and the Fukaya category of the mirror Landau-Ginzburg model. We will then discuss the extension of these results to (essentially arbitrary) complete intersections in toric Fano varieties.

proof_stages.label: Hori-Vafa-type Landau-Ginzburg mirror setup
proof_stages.summary: The speaker fixes smooth complete intersections near a tropical limit in a toric variety, describes the mirror toric Calabi-Yau Landau-Ginzburg model using the tropicalizations of the defining equations, and distinguishes the HMS direction to be pursued from the wrapped-H-versus-DbSing direction.

proof_stages.label: Proposed HMS statement and present status
proof_stages.summary: The target is stated as defining a special fiberwise wrapped/admissible Fukaya category, constructing objects indexed by ambient toric line bundles, and computing their morphisms so as to match Ext groups of the restricted line bundles on the complete intersection, with generation and chain-level refinements still partly conjectural.

proof_stages.label: Admissible Lagrangians and asymptotic flatness
proof_stages.summary: Objects are required to have controlled radial behavior under the main monomial projections and controlled toric-monomial arguments inside the C*^n fibers, giving maximum-principle control and admissibility for the additional toric-potential terms.

proof_stages.label: Base rotation, fiber wrapping, and Floer morphisms
proof_stages.summary: A base flow rotating allowed rays and a fiber Hamiltonian wrapping in toric directions are combined to perturb Lagrangians, and morphisms are defined as a direct limit of Floer complexes that are well-defined because the asymptotic controls separate the ends.

proof_stages.label: Objects from toric line bundles by parallel transport
proof_stages.summary: For each ambient line bundle, Abouzaid's toric mirror Lagrangian graph in the canonical fiber of the main projection is parallel transported along prescribed arcs around the singular value to produce an admissible object of the constructed category.

proof_stages.label: Fiberwise computation of Hom complexes
proof_stages.summary: After perturbation, the base contributes two intersections in each main coordinate, giving 2^k fibers; in each fiber Abouzaid's toric HMS identifies the Floer complexes with Čech models for Ext groups on V, with monodromy corresponding to tensoring by subsets of the defining line bundles.

proof_stages.label: Connecting differentials and the Koszul complex for restriction to H
proof_stages.summary: The differentials between the 2^k fiberwise pieces are computed via holomorphic sections over bigons and Seidel-type TQFT/test-object arguments, yielding multiplication by the defining equations and hence the Koszul/Čech complex computing Ext groups of the line bundles restricted to the complete intersection.

examples.name: Affine higher-dimensional pair of pants
examples.note: The hypersurface x1+...+xn+1=0 in (C*)^n and its mirror C^{n+1} with superpotential minus the product of coordinates are used to make the tropical polytope, the C*^n fibers, admissibility conditions, wrapping, and the canonical fiber concrete, not to supply a proof step.
examples.engagement_reason: he applies the mirror recipe to the affine pair of pants, computing its tropicalization, octant polytope, mirror C^{n+1}, and product superpotential

examples.name: Projective compactification of the pair of pants
examples.note: Compactifying the affine pants to a hyperplane P^{n-1} in P^n illustrates how toric compactification adds the extra monomials t z_i and why the asymptotic argument conditions give admissibility for those terms.
examples.engagement_reason: he compactifies the pants in projective space, homogenizes the equation, identifies the added compactification terms in the superpotential, and compares critical data with the usual mirror

examples.name: Low-dimensional pants admissibility diagram
examples.note: In response to a question, the speaker draws the one-dimensional pair-of-pants mirror and describes moment-map regions where z0, z1, or z2 must have positive real argument, clarifying the abstract asymptotic-flatness definition.
examples.engagement_reason: he draws and reasons through the low-dimensional pants picture, projecting a fiber to R^2 and checking which regions force the coordinates to have real-positive arguments

passes.reason: The low-dimensional pants diagram is the regular three-sector simplex picture for admissibility, serving only as an illustration.

examples.name: Compact and uncompactified toric extremes
examples.note: The cases of compact V and V=(C*)^n are contrasted to illustrate that the same fiberwise perturbation interpolates between a Fukaya-Seidel-type category and ordinary linear wrapping.
examples.engagement_reason: he specializes the fiberwise Hamiltonian/wrapping construction to the compact toric case versus the uncompactified V=(C*)^n case, explaining the resulting Fukaya-side and wrapped-category behaviors
